Data Processing Addendum and PDPA Notice (Singapore) for Project Banyan
Parties: Artemis Pacific Holdings Ltd. (controller; a Lion City Securities Market issuer) and Caldera Capital Partners Pte. Ltd. (processor), with Merlion & Banyan LLC as external counsel.
Purpose: processing personal data and confidential deal materials for a proposed acquisition of Seabright Renewables Pte. Ltd. and related capital-markets and employment integration workstreams.
PDPA compliance: we process contact details, employment records, and device logs about individuals identifiable from that data alone or with other accessible information; we rely on contractual necessity and legitimate interests with consent where required.
MNPI controls: information not generally available and reasonably expected to affect price/value under the SFA and local listing rules will be ring-fenced and used only for the approved purpose.
Insider list: Merlion & Banyan LLC will maintain a contemporaneous insider list for Project Banyan including wall-crossed personnel such as Mr. Koh Jian Ming and Ms. Nur Afiqah, with time stamps and roles.
Information barriers: a clean team at Caldera will handle cyber risk metrics and DPT treasury analytics, segregated from the issuer’s trading personnel; access is chaperoned and recorded.
Blackout and no-tipping: issuer employees are in a pre-deal blackout and must not tip, selectively disclose, or discuss commercial terms with investors or counterparties outside approved channels.
DPT/crypto sensitivity: the target’s confidential stablecoin settlement pilot and a potential exchange listing of a house token are non-public and may be price-sensitive; distribution is restricted to the clean room.
ESG/climate financing: draft terms for a sustainability-linked bond with a 25 bps coupon step-up tied to Scope 2 KPIs are preliminary MNPI and subject to approval; disclosure will follow market announcements.
Cyber incident workflow: if, during diligence, we detect indicators of a ransomware infection beyond a red-team exercise, escalate within 2 hours to the Incident Subcommittee and the DPO for containment and notification assessment.
Data transfers: processors and sub-processors may access data from facilities in Kestrel Isle and Harii Prefecture subject to PDPA transfer obligations, contractual safeguards, and encryption in transit and at rest.
Retention: deal-room materials and insider lists will be retained for seven years after closing or lapse of the transaction, then securely deleted or anonymised unless legal hold applies.
DSARs: individuals may request access, correction, or withdrawal of consent by writing to dpo@artemis-pacific.example; responses will be provided within 30 days unless an exception applies.
Commercial terms confidentiality: non-public pricing concepts (e.g., a 1.15x collar, earn-out bands, and break fee triggers) are MNPI and must not be discussed outside the project team or before approved announcements.
Educational note: our public webinar titled “Insider Lists, Blackouts, and Crypto Controls 101” is a generic training resource and contains no deal-specific or price-sensitive content.
Queries: please contact Mr. Koh Jian Ming via counsel at dealdesk@merlion-banyan.example for clarifications; all communications may be logged for compliance oversight.
